contract.name: v2/download/count.md
contract.description: Counts awards, transactions, or subawards that match advanced download filters and reports whether limits are exceeded.

filters.description: Container for advanced search filters; empty object counts the full dataset.
filters.constraints: Required. Must be a JSON object; missing, null, or non-object values trigger a 500 HTML error.

filters.keywords.description: Array of keyword strings searched across keyword-enabled fields.
filters.keywords.constraints: Must be an array of strings. Sending a string instead returns 400.

filters.time_period.description: List of date range objects constraining the dataset.
filters.time_period.constraints: Each object requires start_date and end_date in YYYY-MM-DD. start_date before 2007-10-01 returns 422; invalid dates return 400.

filters.recipient_search_text.description: Recipient names, DUNS, or UEI strings to match.
filters.recipient_search_text.constraints: Must contain at least one string. Empty array returns 422; multiple entries are accepted.

filters.recipient_locations.description: Recipient location filters using standard location objects (country, state, city, etc.).
filters.recipient_locations.constraints: Entries OR together. Observed forms included state-only and city-only shapes.

filters.recipient_type_names.description: Recipient or business type codes to OR together.
filters.recipient_type_names.constraints: Array of strings such as small_business or individuals.

filters.psc_codes.constraints: Accepts either an array of PSC strings or an object with require/exclude arrays of string arrays. Both styles returned valid counts.

filters.award_amounts.description: Award amount ranges to OR together.
filters.award_amounts.constraints: Each item is an object with optional lower_bound and upper_bound numbers. Inclusivity of bounds remains unverified.

filters.naics_codes.constraints: Optional require and exclude arrays of string prefixes; longer prefixes take precedence when overlapping.

filters.tas_codes.constraints: Optional require/exclude arrays of string arrays representing TAS paths.

filters.treasury_account_components.description: Treasury account component objects (aid, main, optional ata/bpoa/epoa/a/sub).
filters.treasury_account_components.constraints: Observed objects included aid and main; additional optional fields may be supplied per docs.

filters.award_type_codes.description: Award type codes to limit the population.
filters.award_type_codes.constraints: Array of strings; observed values included contract and IDV codes.

spending_level.description: Controls whether counts reflect awards, transactions (default), or subawards.
spending_level.constraints: Enum: awards, transactions, subawards. Missing field defaults to transactions; invalid value returns 400.

calculated_transaction_count.description: Total transactions matching the filters; present for every spending level.

maximum_transaction_limit.description: Current row cap for transaction-based downloads (observed 500000).

calculated_count.description: Primary count aligned with the effective spending_level.

spending_level.description: Echo of the resolved spending_level ('transactions' when omitted).

messages.description: Warning strings; all observed successes returned the same trio covering time period limits, subaward guidance, and upcoming transaction_* renames.

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.
'subawards' will be deprecated in the future. Set ‘spending_level’ to ‘subawards’ instead. See documentation for more information.
The above fields containing the transaction_* naming convention will be deprecated and replaced with fields without the transaction_*.

For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.
'subawards' will be deprecated in the future. Set ‘spending_level’ to ‘subawards’ instead. See documentation for more information.
The above fields containing the transaction_* naming convention will be deprecated and replaced with fields without the transaction_*.

quirks: IPv6 requests completed TLS and returned an empty reply; IPv4 succeeded.
Missing, null, or non-object filters payloads yield generic 500 HTML pages instead of structured validation errors.
Every success response included transaction_* fields plus three warning messages regardless of spending_level or result size.
Counts often exceed the 500000 row limits, flipping *_rows_gt_limit flags to true while still returning totals.
risks: IPv6-only clients may see empty replies; fall back to IPv4 or retry over dual-stack.
Client-side validation is critical because schema violations (missing filters, wrong container type, empty recipient_search_text) produce 500/422 errors.
Downloads will fail if *_rows_gt_limit is true; callers must narrow filters or switch to bulk endpoints.
messages warn that transaction_* fields will be renamed; integrations must watch for schema changes.
gaps: Inclusivity of award_amount lower_bound and upper_bound thresholds remains unverified.
Filters documented for agencies, award_ids, program_numbers, and other fields lack live verification in probes.
Require/exclude precedence rules for PSC and TAS beyond the tested cases were not exhaustively validated.

probes.notes: empty filters object counts entire dataset

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail": "start_date falls before the earliest available search date of 2007-10-01..."}
{"detail":"Invalid value in 'filters|keywords'. 'covid' is not a valid type (array)"}

probes.notes: recipient_search_text requires at least one entry

mismatches: Docs mark filters as required but the API returns an unhelpful 500 HTML page when it is missing or not an object instead of structured validation.
Docs limit recipient_search_text to one value, yet the API accepts multiple entries and instead rejects empty arrays.
Docs say messages are optional, but every observed success included the same warning trio.
gaps: Inclusivity of award_amount lower_bound and upper_bound thresholds remains unverified.
Filters documented for agencies, award_ids, program_numbers, and other fields lack live verification in probes.
Require/exclude precedence rules for PSC and TAS beyond the tested cases were not exhaustively validated.
risks: IPv6-only clients may see empty replies; fall back to IPv4 or retry over dual-stack.
Client-side validation is critical because schema violations (missing filters, wrong container type, empty recipient_search_text) produce 500/422 errors.
Downloads will fail if *_rows_gt_limit is true; callers must narrow filters or switch to bulk endpoints.
messages warn that transaction_* fields will be renamed; integrations must watch for schema changes.
